name: ToDit
tagline: AI productivity system for turning messy thoughts into executable workflows
summary: A productivity platform that converts unstructured inputs into actionable task trees
description: ToDit is an AI-powered productivity platform designed to transform scattered thoughts, images, notes, and documents into structured execution flows. Instead of focusing only on task completion like traditional TODO apps, ToDit externalizes the reasoning process itself. Users can input vague goals or raw ideas, and the inPaser engine recursively breaks them down into actionable subtasks, schedules, and execution paths. Inspired by tools like Workflowy and Obsidian, the project focuses on building executable structures rather than static notes. The product is currently in Lite beta with ongoing experimentation around feedback loops, onboarding, and free/Pro monetization.
homeDescription: A productivity platform that turns scattered thoughts and inputs into executable task trees. Rather than a simple TODO, it focuses on structuring goals into subtasks and schedule flows.

team.contribution: Led planning, product structure design, and frontend implementation

details.problem: Most task managers stop at writing down tasks and checking them off. The hard part is figuring out where and how to break a vague goal into executable steps, and existing tools don't structure that thinking-to-execution process. As a result, users think outside the tool and only copy results back in, repeating an inefficient loop.
details.solution: ToDit interprets unstructured input with the Insight Parser, automatically converting goals into executable task trees and externalizing the goal → subtask → schedule breakdown on a single screen. Instead of recording only results, users can visually handle the flow from thought to execution and clarify their next action.

keyFeatures.title: Unstructured input structuring
keyFeatures.description: Interprets free-form input such as text and images and converts it into executable task units along with context like dates and priorities. Even when users throw in unorganized thoughts, they get a structured starting point.

keyFeatures.title: Task tree
keyFeatures.description: Recursively decomposes a goal into parent and child nodes and visualizes it as a tree, making the hierarchy of thought and execution clear at a glance. Schedule back-calculation connects each node to when it should be executed.

keyFeatures.title: AI-based task suggestions
keyFeatures.description: Based on the input context and tree structure, it structurally suggests the next task or missing steps, reducing where users get stuck and keeping execution continuous.

details.architecture: A Next.js (App Router) and React·TypeScript frontend handles tree interactions and the input UI, while Supabase manages the data, authentication, and real-time sync layers. Unstructured input is processed by the in-house Insight Parser engine, designed as a pipeline of input → interpretation → task tree generation → schedule back-calculation.
details.learnings: We confirmed that a productivity tool's competitiveness depends not on the number of features but on how naturally it captures the user's thinking structure. We also verified firsthand, through the Lite beta feedback loop, that conveying value within three seconds on the first screen determines beta retention.

name: BaekJoon Memorial
tagline: Community memorial platform preserving the final moments of an online coding era
summary: A participatory memorial website built around the shutdown of a major algorithm community
description: Built and deployed within roughly 10 hours after news surrounding the possible shutdown of Baekjoon Online Judge spread through the Korean algorithm community, this memorial website allowed users to leave messages and preserve memories tied to an entire era of competitive programming culture. Because the emotional momentum of the event was highly time-sensitive, speed mattered more than perfection. The platform rapidly spread across PS communities and reached over 100 participants within about 30 hours, eventually receiving media coverage as well. The project became a strong demonstration of fast execution, community timing, and lightweight product delivery.
homeDescription: A participatory memorial website built in about 10 hours so as not to miss the community's brief moment. Through fast deployment and spread, over 100 users left their records.

team.contribution: Project planning, backend infrastructure setup, frontend development, and overall web development

otherMembers.label: rupital - Yoo Chang-yong
otherMembers.contribution: Built the floating comment component and led the overall design

details.problem: News spread that Baekjoon, a space long shared by algorithm learners, might disappear, but there was nowhere to leave behind the memories and emotions of that era. Because mourning sentiment concentrates in the short window while an issue is hot, there was a strong timing constraint—build it too late and the meaning is gone.
details.solution: Aiming to go from planning to deployment in about 10 hours, we built a memorial website focused on a single core feature: letting users log in and leave a final message and record. We stripped away unnecessary features and completed only the participation experience quickly, so it could spread immediately while the issue was alive.

keyFeatures.title: Participatory records
keyFeatures.description: Provides a memorial space where users can leave memories and final messages tied to Baekjoon. The writing flow was simplified so anyone could participate in a short time.

keyFeatures.title: Authenticated writing
keyFeatures.description: NextAuth-based login blocks indiscriminate posting and spam, building a trustworthy participation environment.

keyFeatures.title: Efficient data handling
keyFeatures.description: TanStack Query efficiently fetches and caches surging participation data, maintaining a stable read experience even during traffic spikes.

details.architecture: A structure that combines a Next.js·React·TypeScript frontend with Supabase as the data store and NextAuth as the authentication layer, managing server state and caching with TanStack Query. To deploy quickly, the components were minimized using a familiar stack.
details.learnings: Through rapid development, deployment, and community spread, I experienced firsthand that in timing-critical projects, shipping now creates more value than polish. At the same time, I confirmed that data handling and authentication design under traffic spikes determine real service reliability.

metrics.label: Development
metrics.value: Built and deployed in a short time

name: ESSENTIA Science
tagline: Integrated operating platform for student research organizations
summary: Web-based platform centralizing member, project, forum, and resource management
description: ESSENTIA Science is a web-based organizational management platform designed for student-led research communities. Rather than functioning as a simple homepage, the system integrates member onboarding, organizational structure, permissions, project tracking, forums, voting systems, and archives into a single operational platform. The product translates real-world organizational workflows into software architecture, reducing fragmentation caused by scattered tools and enabling long-term operational continuity.
homeDescription: An operating platform that ties a student research organization's members, projects, forum, voting, and archive into one. It moves the real organizational flow into product structure to unify management and records.

team.contribution: Designed the web product structure and handled the full implementation

details.problem: The student research organization was run with its homepage, member roster, project records, and shared resources scattered across different tools. When information is fragmented, handover is hard, it's difficult to trace who did what with which permission, and operating costs rise steeply as the organization grows.
details.solution: We built an integrated management platform that runs members, organization, projects, forum, voting, and archives on a single permission system. By mapping the organization's actual work units directly to features, the staff can manage the organization within one system without separate tools.

keyFeatures.title: Member & organization management
keyFeatures.description: Integrates member registration, roles and permissions, and organizational structure. Access is separated by permission so operations don't break down as the organization grows.

keyFeatures.title: Projects & forum
keyFeatures.description: Runs research project progress alongside forum-based discussion spaces so activity and records continue within the same context.

keyFeatures.title: Voting & archive
keyFeatures.description: Provides voting for organizational decisions and a resource-sharing archive, accumulating both the decision process and the outputs in one place.

details.architecture: A React·TypeScript frontend and Node.js backend structure the permission and data flow, with AI integrated into some features to improve operational convenience. The information structure was designed so members, organization, projects, and resources connect on a single data model.
details.learnings: While translating a real organization's operational flow into product structure, I learned that requirement organization and information structure design—more than feature implementation—determine a system's success. Since the users are operators, a consistent permission model and clear information hierarchy mattered more than flashy features.

metrics.label: Scope
metrics.value: Members, organization, projects, forum, voting, and archive integrated

name: ICAROS
tagline: Aerospace research project focused on UAV systems and simulation
summary: Exploratory aerospace project centered on UAV design, flight control, and simulation
description: ICAROS is an aerospace exploration project focused on UAV design and flight control systems. The project separates its public-facing research channel from its simulation environment, using icaros.kr as a documentation and branding platform while operating sim.icaros.kr as an isolated experimental space for simulation and control validation. The long-term vision is to build foundations related to autonomous mobility and intelligent control systems through aerospace-oriented experimentation.

team.contribution: Set the direction for UAV design and control research and built the simulation platform

details.problem: UAV design and flight control carry high cost and risk when accessing real systems, so an environment to safely validate hypotheses before physical testing is needed. In addition, when the research process and outputs are scattered, the context of exploration doesn't accumulate.
details.solution: By separating the main site for UAV technology exploration from the simulation subdomain, the research narrative and the experimental environment were structurally divided. Flight and control scenarios are repeatedly validated in the simulation space, while the process and results accumulate on the main channel.

keyFeatures.title: UAV technology exploration
keyFeatures.description: Researches core elements of aerospace technology centered on UAV design and flight control, laying a foundation to expand into mobility and control systems.

keyFeatures.title: Simulation space
keyFeatures.description: Through sim.icaros.kr, operates an independent experimental space for validating flight and control scenarios before physical testing.

keyFeatures.title: Project branding
keyFeatures.description: Centered on icaros.kr, organizes the research direction, progress, and outputs into a single brand narrative to accumulate the context of exploration.

details.architecture: A web-based structure that separates the main domain (icaros.kr) from the simulation subdomain (sim.icaros.kr), designed so the research-content area and the experimental area can be operated and expanded independently.
details.learnings: I organized the insight that a structure accumulating research context, rather than one-off experiments, holds greater value in long-term exploration, and I laid a foundation to broaden the project's spectrum into mobility and control systems through the aerospace domain.

name: ON-Alim
tagline: AI-powered assistant helping seniors convert daily information into action
summary: Senior-focused AI assistant that turns fragmented life information into actionable reminders
description: ON-Alim is an AI reminder assistant designed to reduce the cognitive burden seniors face when processing medicine labels, bills, schedules, and smartphone notifications. Using OCR, voice input, and notification parsing, the app extracts structured information such as time, location, medication schedules, and priorities through the Insight Parser engine. Instead of merely displaying information, the system focuses on transforming information into concrete daily actions through strong reminders and simplified execution flows.

team.contribution: Defined the problem, designed the app structure, implemented it in Flutter, and designed the unstructured-information parsing flow

otherMembers.label: Kim Chan-woo
otherMembers.contribution: Researched senior usage scenarios and prepared the competition presentation

details.problem: Amid small text, complex bills, medication schedules, and a flood of smartphone notifications, seniors struggle to read, remember, and act on what to do and when. The information is already in hand, but they most often miss the final step of turning it into action.
details.solution: Daily information is captured via photo, voice, and notification collection, AI interprets the key information, and the result is turned into a today's to-do list with hard-to-miss strong reminders. The app structures the read-remember-act burden on the user's behalf, so all they need to do is check what to do today.

keyFeatures.title: Daily information input
keyFeatures.description: Captures medicine labels, bills, and notifications through photos, voice, and notification collection—methods seniors can use comfortably—lowering the entry barrier of the input stage itself.

keyFeatures.title: AI information parsing
keyFeatures.description: Using the inPaser engine, it extracts date, time, place, amount, medication, and priority, structuring unstructured daily information into actionable data.

keyFeatures.title: Today's to-do conversion
keyFeatures.description: Organizes the parsed information into a today's to-do list that seniors can act on immediately, with strong reminders and voice guidance that connect through to execution.

details.architecture: Centered on an Android app built with Flutter·Dart, it places an interpretation layer combining OCR, an LLM, and the in-house inPaser engine, composing a flow of input → interpretation → today's to-do → completion record along with terms and history screens.
details.learnings: I confirmed that for social-impact apps, UX design that actually carries the user through to action—more than technical sophistication—determines success. Especially for seniors, designing by removing steps is harder and more important than adding features.

awards.title: Dynamic Dankook Startup Idea Competition (High School Division)
awards.description: Senior reminder-to-action app concept and MVP

name: PatentSense
tagline: Agentic AI workflow for patent similarity analysis and idea validation
summary: LangGraph-based AI agent automating patent search, ranking, and analysis
description: PatentSense is an AI agent that converts natural-language startup ideas into technical patent queries, retrieves related Korean patents from KIPRIS, and generates structured similarity reports using Solar Embeddings and LLM-based reasoning. Instead of relying on a single LLM call, the system is designed as an agentic workflow with separated stages for retrieval, ranking, fallback handling, and report generation. Developed as part of the AI·SW Maestro 17th technical training project, the system received positive feedback for its workflow architecture and implementation quality.

team.contribution: Provided feedback and testing on the agentic workflow structure and contributed to frontend enhancements and UI improvements

otherMembers.label: Bae In-soo
otherMembers.contribution: Handled proposal writing, presentation materials, project documentation, and the overall presentation flow

otherMembers.label: Jung Young-in
otherMembers.contribution: Supported backend implementation and managed GitHub collaboration rules and project operations

otherMembers.label: Jung Je-il
otherMembers.contribution: Played a core role in overall system implementation, focusing on agentic workflow implementation and testing

details.problem: When reviewing startup or invention ideas, it's hard to quickly gauge similarity to existing patents. Patent search requires understanding precise technical terms and classification systems, raising the entry barrier, and it takes a non-expert a long time to judge whether their idea already exists.
details.solution: It converts natural-language ideas into technical search keywords and automates KIPRIS patent retrieval → embedding similarity ranking → LLM report generation as a single agentic workflow. Users simply write their idea in everyday language and receive a report covering similar patents, conflict points, and differentiation strategy.

keyFeatures.title: Keyword extraction
keyFeatures.description: Converts an idea written in everyday language into technical keywords suited to patent search, so users can start an accurate search even without expert search knowledge.

keyFeatures.title: Patent retrieval & ranking
keyFeatures.description: Collects related Korean patents via the KIPRIS Plus API and sorts them by Solar Embedding-based similarity to surface the five most similar results.

keyFeatures.title: Analysis report generation
keyFeatures.description: Generates a Korean markdown report including conflict points and differentiation strategy against similar patents, providing an output ready for decision-making.

details.architecture: A linear agent workflow built with LangGraph separates search, retrieval, ranking, analysis, and fallback, integrating a FastAPI·Python backend, a Next.js/Streamlit UI, the KIPRIS Plus API, and Solar LLM·Embedding. Docker Compose bundles the runtime, and SSE delivers progress in real time.
details.learnings: I learned that an agentic workflow separating search, ranking, analysis, and exception handling into stages greatly improves the completeness and reliability of results compared to a single LLM call. Placing a fallback at each stage proved decisive for stability in real usage.

metrics.label: Coach Feedback
metrics.value: Positive evaluation of implementation quality and Agentic Workflow understanding

name: Safe Ground
tagline: Traffic safety application visualizing accident-prone zones with public data
summary: Map-based safety application combining T-map APIs and public accident datasets
description: Safe Ground is a public-safety application that visualizes high-risk traffic accident areas around schools using T-map APIs and Korean public accident datasets. By converting statistical accident records into intuitive map overlays and risk-level color systems, the application helps students recognize dangerous routes in their daily commute. The project focused on transforming underutilized public data into actionable safety information and won the Grand Prize at the DKSH Safety App Challenge.

team.contribution: Handled app planning, Flutter implementation, public data integration, and risk-level visualization

details.problem: Even on roads they travel every day, students find it hard to intuitively recognize which areas around school are accident-prone. Accident statistics are open as public data, but in table and figure form they're hard to feel in connection with actual walking routes.
details.solution: By combining public accident data with a map API, accident-prone areas are visualized on the map in risk-level colors, providing safety information so users can immediately grasp dangerous segments along their own routes.

keyFeatures.title: Accident hotspot visualization
keyFeatures.description: Displays traffic accident data directly on the map, turning statistics into risk areas tied to walking routes for intuitive understanding.

keyFeatures.title: Risk-level color coding
keyFeatures.description: Distinguishes colors by risk level in stages so users can quickly recognize the degree of danger just by scanning the map.

keyFeatures.title: Map API integration
keyFeatures.description: Combines the T-map API with K-Road accident data to build a trustworthy safety map on the actual road network.

details.architecture: A structure where a Flutter·Dart app integrates the T-map API and public accident data, processes the collected data by risk level, and renders it through a map-based visualization UI.
details.learnings: I felt firsthand that combining open-but-underused public data with APIs and visualization turns it into a tool that solves real-life safety problems. I confirmed the importance of designing data to be connected to action rather than merely shown.

awards.title: 2025 Safety App Development Challenge
awards.description: Safe Ground, a safety app visualizing traffic accident risk zones

name: OBD-II CAN Tester
tagline: Real-time automotive diagnostic and CAN visualization tool
summary: OBD-II diagnostic platform visualizing live vehicle telemetry and hybrid states
description: OBD-II CAN Tester is a real-time automotive diagnostic tool that asynchronously collects six different PID values from vehicle OBD-II systems, including RPM, speed, intake temperature, throttle position, MAF, and coolant temperature. Built with Python, Tkinter, and Matplotlib, the system visualizes live telemetry data while inferring EV, Hybrid, and Stop driving states based on vehicle behavior patterns. The project was tested on a real 2012 Toyota Prius and reflects a broader interest in mobility systems and real-time data processing.

team.contribution: Handled OBD-II data collection, asynchronous polling, the real-time graph UI, and the hybrid-state detection logic

details.problem: A wealth of data flows inside a vehicle, but it's hard for drivers or developers to check it in real time and interpret the driving state. The values accessible via OBD-II are raw, so reading them as meaningful states requires separate processing and visualization.
details.solution: It asynchronously collects six key PIDs from the OBD-II port and visualizes the vehicle state through real-time graphs and a hybrid-state detection UI. The collected raw data is interpreted with domain rules to convert it into human-readable states like EV mode, engine running, or stopped.

keyFeatures.title: Asynchronous PID collection
keyFeatures.description: Uses obd.Async to continuously and periodically collect six vehicle metrics such as RPM and speed, designed so collection latency doesn't block the UI.

keyFeatures.title: Real-time graphs
keyFeatures.description: A Matplotlib-based 3x2 graph refreshes each PID at one-second intervals, making it possible to observe the changing vehicle state in real time while driving.

keyFeatures.title: Hybrid-state detection
keyFeatures.description: Infers EV MODE, Hybrid MODE, and Stop states from the combination of RPM and speed, interpreting raw values as states in a driving context.

details.architecture: data.py handles the OBD connection and collection layer, ui.py the Tkinter·Matplotlib-based GUI, and main.py the threading structure that bridges the two. Collection and rendering are separated to satisfy both real-time needs and the GUI main-thread constraint.
details.learnings: I gained experience handling a hardware interface, real-time data polling, the GUI main-thread constraint, and domain-based state interpretation all within one project. In particular, I confirmed that domain logic translating raw data into meaningful states creates more value than mere collection.

metrics.label: Test Environment
metrics.value: Tested on a real 2012 Toyota Prius

name: Witch
tagline: Team-based elemental wave defense game built with pygame
summary: 2D strategy defense game featuring elemental combat and wave systems
description: Witch is a 2D wave-defense game where players place elemental spirits onto a 4x6 battlefield grid to defend against incoming monsters. The project includes elemental affinity systems, wave progression, scene transitions, animation state machines, and executable packaging through PyInstaller. Developed as a four-person team project, the work focused heavily on development pipeline organization, Git collaboration workflows, and data structuring to improve team scalability and parallel development.

team.contribution: Handled development pipeline management, Git version control, data structuring, and organizing the team's development flow

otherMembers.label: Hwang Ji-sung
otherMembers.contribution: Designed the game's base structure and developed the gameplay logic

otherMembers.label: Jung Se-jin
otherMembers.contribution: Created graphic assets and illustration and visual resources

otherMembers.label: Park Woo-won
otherMembers.contribution: Handled game planning, world-building, story writing, and narrative design

details.problem: In team game development, code, image/sound assets, wave data, and build artifacts quickly get mixed up. Without organization, conflicts become frequent and it gets hard to trace who changed what, sharply raising collaboration and maintenance costs.
details.solution: By organizing a Git branch strategy, data-file-based wave configuration, consistent asset-loading rules, and a PyInstaller deployment structure, the team-based game development flow was systematized. Separating content (wave/element data) from logic let teammates develop in parallel while encroaching less on each other's work.

keyFeatures.title: 4x6 grid combat
keyFeatures.description: Implements strategic placement combat where players drag spirits onto the grid and defend against monsters entering row by row.

keyFeatures.title: Element-based system
keyFeatures.description: Applies six elements—water, fire, grass, light, stone, dark—to spirits and monsters, adding affinity strategy to placement choices.

keyFeatures.title: Wave & scene structure
keyFeatures.description: Supports story_wave.json-based data-driven waves and start·story·game·ending scene transitions, so content can be adjusted via data without code changes.

details.architecture: A pygame structure centered on the Game class in main.py, combined with supporting modules such as spirit, monster, effect, button, and mana; wave and element data are separated into external files to apply a data-driven design.
details.learnings: While directly handling the real-time game loop, state machines, and data-driven wave design, I experienced that in team development, organizing the Git workflow and the data/asset structure matters more to collaboration speed than the code itself.

awards.title: National Youth Open SW GAME Coding Competition (with Hanyang University)

name: Aiden Portfolio
tagline: Minimal portfolio platform presenting projects through product storytelling
summary: Personal portfolio website organizing projects, achievements, and growth into a unified narrative
description: Aiden Portfolio is a minimal dark-themed portfolio website designed around product storytelling rather than simple project listings. Structured into Hero, Journey, Projects, and Elsewhere sections, the platform focuses on connecting projects into a coherent narrative centered on execution, product building, and long-term growth. Project data is managed through JSON-driven structures to enable scalable expansion and maintain consistency across the platform.

team.contribution: Handled the branding direction, section structure, project data structure, and frontend implementation

details.problem: When various projects, awards, and activities are scattered, each may be impressive yet fail to convey a single positioning of what this person does. The more information there is, the more the core narrative tends to blur.
details.solution: With product-centered storytelling and a restrained minimal UI, it selectively shows only the core projects and growth flow. By connecting the year-by-year journey with representative projects, it organizes scattered outputs into one career narrative.

keyFeatures.title: Journey Timeline
keyFeatures.description: Organizes the year-by-year growth flow and key turning points to show the context of how I developed, not just the outputs.

keyFeatures.title: Project System
keyFeatures.description: Manages projects on a JSON-data basis and designs them to expand into detail pages, keeping a consistent structure even as content grows.

keyFeatures.title: Minimal Dark UI
keyFeatures.description: A black background, limited accent colors, and restrained motion create an impression of quiet confidence, focusing attention on the content.

details.architecture: On a Next.js·React·TypeScript static/dynamic section structure, Framer Motion adds restrained interactions, deployed via Vercel with the domain managed through Cloudflare DNS. Project data is separated into JSON for a data-driven rendering structure.
details.learnings: I organized the insight that a portfolio is not a page that lists a lot of information but a single product that clearly shows the flow and positioning between projects. I confirmed firsthand through design that what you leave out matters as much as what you put in.
